attacking_style: Does not initiate attacks; relies on fast finish in bunch sprints when gradients are minimal. Opportunistic sprinter who capitalizes on depleted fields rather than creating opportunities.
team_tactics: Quick-Step provides traditional sprint train support, though Vernon has shown ability to win without full leadout when field is reduced. Team positioning appears standard for sprint preparation.
terrain_preference: Flat to minimal gradient finishes where pure speed decides. Struggles on late climbs or drag finishes that require sustained power over variable terrain.
vulnerability: Late-race climbs and drag finishes expose clear climbing deficit. Cannot contest sprints when even moderate gradients thin the bunch, despite team support.
key_patterns: Dropped on late drag finishes, preventing sprint contest despite team support (2 observations)
Wins sprints from reduced/depleted fields rather than full bunch scenarios (2 observations)
Can finish without traditional leadout, suggesting superior raw speed over positioning dependency (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: Showed speed at Catalunya in March but climbing weakness exposed at Romandie in April prevented finishing
